title: pluchea viscida
intro: Pluchea viscida, also known as sticky pluchea, is a perennial plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, Australia, and the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pluchea viscida include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots are often used in traditional remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
